name: LG 65" G4 OLED evo 4K Smart TV (2024)

specs.Panel Type: OLED evo with Micro Lens Array (MLA), WRGB
specs.HDR Support: Dolby Vision IQ, HDR10, HLG, FILMMAKER MODE
specs.Gaming: 4K 144Hz VRR, G-Sync Compatible, FreeSync Premium, ALLM, 0.1ms
specs.Audio: Dolby Atmos, DTS:X, AI Sound Pro

whatWorks: Second-generation Micro Lens Array delivers around 1,650 nits peak brightness in calibrated mode — roughly 15–18% brighter than the G3 — and the improvement is visible in HDR highlights during demanding content like outdoor sports and explosions.

Perfect black levels remain the defining advantage: nearly every owner review and professional test singles out the G4's contrast as the reason they chose it over any mini-LED competitor — the ink-black letterboxing on widescreen films and dark-scene detail in games like Dead Space are consistently described as revelatory.

Gaming spec sheet is among the most complete available: 4K at up to 144Hz in VRR, 0.1ms response time, G-Sync Compatible, FreeSync Premium, ALLM, and four full HDMI 2.1 ports — PS5, Xbox Series X, PC, and a fourth input simultaneously with no compromise.

Colour accuracy out of the box is a recurring topic in positive reviews — the G4's Dolby Vision IQ implementation and webOS calibration tools are widely regarded as the most accurate factory settings in any 4K TV at this price.
worthKnowing: OLED burn-in risk remains an ownership consideration — LG's 5-year warranty includes burn-in coverage on the G4, but owners who display static content (news tickers, PC desktops, sports overlays) for extended periods should use the built-in pixel maintenance tools diligently.

The G4 is noticeably dimmer than premium mini-LED TVs in very bright rooms — while 1,650 nits is strong for OLED, dedicated bright-room setups facing windows may prefer the Samsung QN90D for daytime HDR punch.
webOS 24's interface is more cluttered than previous generations — a recurring topic in owner threads who note the recommendation algorithm has become more aggressive in surfacing sponsored content.

commonComplaints.title: Burn-in risk is a consistent topic…
commonComplaints.body: Burn-in risk is a consistent topic in the LG 2024 OLED owners thread on AVSForum — while the G4 includes a 5-year warranty with burn-in coverage, owners of previous G-series models have reported accelerated retention from gaming with static HUDs.

commonComplaints.title: Banding on grey-gradient test patterns is…
commonComplaints.body: Banding on grey-gradient test patterns is a known characteristic of WRGB OLED panels — multiple owners in the G4 thread note visible horizontal banding in near-black and uniform grey scenes, which is inherent to the panel type but unexpected at this price.

commonComplaints.title: The webOS smart platform has become…
commonComplaints.body: The webOS smart platform has become slower and more ad-driven with each update — owners in r/4kTV consistently note that the home screen now surfaces LG Channels and sponsored content more aggressively than webOS 22/23.

verdict: Owner sentiment on the LG G4 is as close to consensus as premium TVs get — picture quality, black levels, and gaming performance earn near-universal praise across forum threads and review aggregators. The most common caveat from experienced owners is the burn-in discussion, which the 5-year warranty partially addresses, and the brightness ceiling compared to mini-LED alternatives in very bright rooms. For dark-room viewing, home theatre use, or gaming where image fidelity matters more than raw brightness, nearly every owner review rates the G4 as the best TV available at its price.

realBuyers.quote: The black levels changed my understanding of what TV can look like. Games in dark environments look like a completely different format. My LG C2 before this was good. This is better.
Four HDMI 2.1 with 4K 144Hz is the thing nobody talks about enough. My PS5, Xbox, PC, and soundbar all connected simultaneously. No switching, no compromises.
Coming from a Samsung QLED. Colours are more natural on the G4 — less saturated but more accurate. Dolby Vision on Netflix looks like a different stream than what I was watching before.

sources.title: LG G4 OLED TV Review — OLED Goes Off the Charts in 2024 | Digital Trends

metaDescription: LG G4 OLED 65" review for Canada. MLA OLED, 4K 144Hz gaming, Dolby Vision IQ at $3,499 CAD. Honest look at burn-in risk, brightness limits, and who it's for.
introText: The LG G4 is LG's flagship 4K OLED for 2024, stepping above the C4 with a second-generation Micro Lens Array panel that boosts peak brightness to around 1,650 nits in calibrated mode. It combines perfect OLED black levels with a complete gaming spec sheet — four HDMI 2.1 ports at 144Hz — and Dolby Vision IQ for the most accurate streaming HDR available. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B0D3WY2FHF).
